title: H-Index
hints: Notice the definition depends only on counts, not specific paper IDs. How does sorting citations help you count papers with at least k citations efficiently?
Consider using a frequency array (bucket sort) since citation values are bounded. Count how many papers have each citation count, then accumulate from highest to lowest.
The h-index is the largest h where the accumulated count of papers with >= h citations is at least h. Check these counts in descending order of h.
